content.text: The Modify effect mutates resource properties or tags at request time — injecting, replacing, or removing values before the resource is persisted. Unlike Append, Modify supports three distinct operations (addOrReplace, add, remove), can correct wrong values rather than only filling absent ones, and supports remediation tasks to retroactively apply changes to existing non-compliant resources. Modify requires a managed identity on the policy assignment because remediation tasks make separate API calls against existing resources using that identity's credentials.

attrs.alt: Modify effect: on-create mutation and remediation task flow diagram

content.text: A Modify policy's then.details.operations array specifies one or more mutation operations. Each operation has an operation type, a field path, and (for add/addOrReplace) a value. Multiple operations can be combined in a single policy definition.
Sets the field to the value
Replaces existing value with policy value
Sets the field to the value
Removes the field regardless of value
Add or replace a required tag

Modify requires a managed identity on the policy assignment. The identity is used both for on-create mutations (the identity token is used to make the PATCH call on behalf of the original requestor) and for remediation task deployments against existing resources. The identity must have the role specified in the roleDefinitionIds array at the assignment scope — typically Contributor.

# Create Modify assignment with system-assigned managed identity
az policy assignment create \
  --name add-cost-center-tag \
  --management-group mg-corp \
  --policy $MODIFY_POLICY_DEF_ID \
  --mi-system-assigned \
  --location eastus2 \
  --params '{"costCenter":{"value":"unassigned"}}'

# Get the managed identity object ID
IDENTITY_OID=$(az policy assignment show \
  --name add-cost-center-tag \
  --scope /providers/Microsoft.Management/managementGroups/mg-corp \
  --query 'identity.principalId' -o tsv)

# Grant the identity Contributor at the same scope as the assignment
az role assignment create \
  --assignee $IDENTITY_OID \
  --role Contributor \
  --scope /providers/Microsoft.Management/managementGroups/mg-corp

# Confirm assignment and identity are configured
az policy assignment show \
  --name add-cost-center-tag \
  --scope /providers/Microsoft.Management/managementGroups/mg-corp \
  --query '{Display:displayName,IdentityType:identity.type,PrincipalId:identity.principalId}'

Modify policies only apply their mutations when a resource is created or updated after the assignment was made. Resources that existed before the assignment are marked non-compliant but not automatically updated. A remediation task runs the Modify operation against existing non-compliant resources using the assignment's managed identity.

# Check how many existing resources are non-compliant (would receive the tag)
az policy state list \
  --management-group mg-corp \
  --filter "policyAssignmentName eq 'add-cost-center-tag' and complianceState eq 'NonCompliant'" \
  --query 'length(@)' -o tsv

# Create a remediation task to apply the Modify operation to existing resources
az policy remediation create \
  --name remediate-cost-center-tag \
  --policy-assignment /providers/Microsoft.Management/managementGroups/mg-corp/providers/Microsoft.Authorization/policyAssignments/add-cost-center-tag \
  --management-group mg-corp \
  --resource-discovery-mode ReEvaluateCompliance

# Monitor remediation progress
az policy remediation show \
  --name remediate-cost-center-tag \
  --management-group mg-corp \
  --query '{Status:provisioningState,Total:deploymentSummary.totalDeployments,Success:deploymentSummary.successfulDeployments,Failed:deploymentSummary.failedDeployments}'

# After remediation completes, trigger compliance rescan
az policy state trigger-scan \
  --subscription $PROD_SUB_ID

# Verify resources are now compliant
az policy state list \
  --management-group mg-corp \
  --filter "policyAssignmentName eq 'add-cost-center-tag' and complianceState eq 'NonCompliant'" \
  --query 'length(@)' -o tsv
# Should be 0 after successful remediation

The depends_on on the role assignment is required because the managed identity's principal_id is only known after the policy assignment is created. Without depends_on, Terraform may attempt to plan the role assignment before the principal_id exists, causing an error.
Multiple Operations in One Modify Policy

A single Modify policy can combine multiple operations. For example, a comprehensive tag policy might add the cost-center tag, add the environment tag, and remove a disallowed employee-email tag — all in the same policy. Each operation is evaluated and applied independently; if the resource already satisfies an operation's target state, that operation is a no-op.

Every mutation performed by a Modify effect (on-create or via remediation task) writes an entry to the Azure Activity Log. The requestor shown in the log is the policy managed identity — not the original user or service principal that created the resource. When reviewing activity logs to understand why a resource has a particular tag, searching for entries from the policy managed identity's principal ID reveals Modify-driven changes.

# Find the managed identity's principal ID for a Modify assignment
IDENTITY_OID=$(az policy assignment show \
  --name add-cost-center-tag \
  --scope /providers/Microsoft.Management/managementGroups/mg-corp \
  --query 'identity.principalId' -o tsv)

# Search activity log for mutations made by this identity in a specific RG
az monitor activity-log list \
  --resource-group rg-prod \
  --caller $IDENTITY_OID \
  --start-time $(date -u -d '7 days ago' +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ 2>/dev/null || date -u -v-7d +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ) \
  --query '[].{Time:eventTimestamp,Op:operationName.value,Status:status.value,Resource:resourceId}' \
  -o table

# Query Resource Graph for policy-driven tag changes
az graph query -q "
ResourceChanges
| where properties.changeType == 'Update'
| where properties.changedBy contains '$IDENTITY_OID'
| where properties.changes['tags.cost-center'].changeCategory == 'User'
| project timestamp, resourceId, before = properties.changes['tags.cost-center'].previousValue,
    after = properties.changes['tags.cost-center'].newValue
| order by timestamp desc
"

# Verify remediation task results
az policy remediation deployment list \
  --name remediate-cost-center-tag \
  --management-group mg-corp \
  --query '[].{Resource:resourceId,Status:provisioningState}' \
  -o table
Common Modify Patterns for Tag Governance
Add or correct regardless of current
resourceGroup().tags['x'] notEquals '' AND tag exists = false

# Assign the comprehensive tag Modify policy
az policy assignment create \
  --name corp-tag-governance \
  --management-group mg-corp \
  --policy $MULTI_OP_POLICY_DEF_ID \
  --mi-system-assigned \
  --location eastus2 \
  --params '{
    "costCenter":{"value":"unassigned"},
    "environment":{"value":"unknown"}
  }'

# Grant Contributor to the managed identity
IDENTITY_OID=$(az policy assignment show \
  --name corp-tag-governance \
  --scope /providers/Microsoft.Management/managementGroups/mg-corp \
  --query 'identity.principalId' -o tsv)

az role assignment create \
  --assignee $IDENTITY_OID \
  --role Contributor \
  --scope /providers/Microsoft.Management/managementGroups/mg-corp

# Run remediation to fix all existing non-compliant resources
az policy remediation create \
  --name remediate-corp-tags \
  --policy-assignment /providers/Microsoft.Management/managementGroups/mg-corp/providers/Microsoft.Authorization/policyAssignments/corp-tag-governance \
  --management-group mg-corp \
  --resource-discovery-mode ReEvaluateCompliance

# Check remediation progress periodically
WATCH_CMD='az policy remediation show --name remediate-corp-tags --management-group mg-corp --query "{Status:provisioningState,Total:deploymentSummary.totalDeployments,Success:deploymentSummary.successfulDeployments}"'
echo $WATCH_CMD
eval $WATCH_CMD
Modify vs DINE: Property Changes vs Resource Deployment

Modify and DeployIfNotExists (DINE) are both effects that make changes to Azure resources using a managed identity. The key distinction is what they change. Modify changes properties and tags on the resource that triggered the policy evaluation — it is a PATCH operation against the existing resource. DINE creates or updates a companion resource (an extension, a diagnostic setting, a role assignment) as a separate ARM deployment. Use Modify for tag enforcement, property correction, and resource configuration updates. Use DINE when a separate resource must be created as a side effect (installing a VM extension, creating a diagnostic setting, deploying a policy assignment).
PATCH call against the existing resource
Yes (for remediation tasks and on-create mutations)
Extension installation, diagnostic settings, private endpoints

The Modify effect mutates resource properties and tags at request time (addOrReplace, add, or remove operations) and supports remediation tasks to retroactively apply changes to existing non-compliant resources. Unlike Append, Modify can correct wrong values (not only fill in absent ones) using addOrReplace, and can remove fields using the remove operation. Modify requires a system-assigned or user-assigned managed identity on the assignment; the identity needs the role specified in roleDefinitionIds (typically Contributor) at the assignment scope. Remediation tasks run asynchronously using the managed identity — always verify the identity has the correct RBAC before creating a remediation task across a large scope.
